raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX airline tickets were purchased from XXXX totaling {$6200.00} ( each costing {$3100.00} ). On XX/XX/XXXX, both tickets were cancelled on XXXX XXXX website after consulting with their policy as well as the XXXX Department of Transportation rule of cancelling tickets within XXXX hours and receiving a full refund. Prior to cancelling, I tried to contact XXXX to verify I would receive a full refund ; however, their call queue was so long that it would not accept anyone else to wait and disconnected. I also tried to chat with a representative on the website ; however, that too had too many customers waiting. XXXX thus only refunded the taxes and fees which totaled {$310.00} for both tickets. When purchasing a ticket on XXXX 's website, it clearly states, FARE IS FULLY REFUNDABLE WITHIN XXXX HOURS AFTER RESERVATIONS AND TICKETING DATE. '' The interpretation I have of this is XXXX hours after the DATE of the ticket and since my ticket was purchased on XX/XX/XXXX and cancelled on XX/XX/XXXX, I should receive a full refund. XXXX stated to Citibank that it needed to be cancelled within XXXX hours of the actual time purchased. Even though it was pointed out that their rules differ from this, XXXX will not refund the remaining balance. 

XXXX disputes were created with Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX, but they could not resolve the issue with XXXX. Additional documentation was provided by me to Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX clearly showing that the rule state FARE IS FULLY REFUNDABLE WITHIN XXXX HOURS AFTER RESERVATIONS AND TICKETING DATE '' ; however, XXXX would not honor it.
